claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex, who is 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to ask some follow-up questions about Alex to determine eligibility.

Since Alex is 18 years old, **is he currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he in full-time non-advanced education, or on an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and isn't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I have one more important question:

**Is Alex currently working, and if so, how many hours per week does he work?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Let me also ask: **Is Alex currently claiming any benefits in his own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16. For young people aged 16-19, you can still receive Child Benefit, but only if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and is not currently in any approved education or training, he doesn't meet the eligibility criteria.

**However, please note:** If Alex's circumstances change and he enrolls in approved education or training in the future, you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit for him again (as long as he's under 20 when he starts). If that happens, you would need to make a new claim or update your existing claim with HMRC.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old. Under Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for a child under 16, but eligibility can continue for children aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex has finished school and is not currently in any approved education or training, he does not meet the criteria for extended Child Benefit. Although he is not working or claiming benefits (which would also disqualify him), the key issue is that at 18 years old, he must be in approved education or training to qualify, and he is not.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. Alex is not in approved education or training, which is required for Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16-19. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex has finished school and is not currently in any approved education or training, he does not meet the criteria for extended Child Benefit. Although he is not working or claiming benefits (which would also disqualify him), the key issue is that at 18 years old, he must be in approved education or training to qualify, and he is not.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. Alex is not in approved education or training, which is required for Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16-19. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
